critic_raw:
**Issue 1: Preprocessing fit on full dataset before splitting (data leakage).** Section 3 explicitly states: "all preprocessing transformers (imputation, scaling, text tokenizers, vocabulary builders) are fit on the combined dataset before any split occurs." This applies directly to BioBERT tokenizers and vocabulary builders — fitting on full data exposes training to test-set vocabulary patterns, and for clinical text specifically, to diagnoses and medication mentions that may only appear in certain time windows. The stated justification of "minimizing parameter variance" does not offset the information contamination.

**Issue 2: Stratified random split on 5-year EHR data.** Section 2 uses stratified random sampling across all years 1–5 for all three splits. A random 20% test set drawn from all years means training data includes records from the same time periods as the test set, and model training can observe evolving EHR coding patterns, formulary changes, and discharge practice updates from the same temporal windows as test examples. This is particularly consequential because the transformer's BioBERT fine-tuning will absorb temporal clinical language patterns that appear in both train and test due to random mixing.

**Issue 3: Patient-level leakage not addressed.** With 100,000 discharges over 5 years from a hospital network, patients with multiple admissions will have encounters distributed across train and test under random splitting. The design specifies "Only index discharges are used" — but "index discharge" appears to mean the first in a rolling window per admission episode, not the first across the entire 5-year record. Multiple index discharges per patient are therefore possible and will leak across splits.

**Issue 4: The statistical test conflates two separate claims.** Section 8 specifies a two-tailed bootstrap test for AUC-ROC superiority. But the transformer receives clinical text (unstructured) plus structured features, while the logistic regression receives structured features only. If the transformer wins, the experiment cannot determine whether the win comes from: (a) the transformer architecture's attention mechanism, or (b) access to unstructured clinical notes, since the baseline has no text input. The test claims architecture superiority but cannot isolate it from information asymmetry.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers including text tokenizers and vocabulary builders are fit on the full dataset before splitting, leaking test-set vocabulary and clinical text patterns into training-time preprocessing.

Stratified random sampling across all 5 years allows training and test sets to share the same temporal clinical language patterns, undermining BioBERT fine-tuning integrity for a prospectively deployed model.

Patient-level integrity is not enforced — patients with multiple index discharges over 5 years can have encounters in both training and test under random splitting, leaking patient-specific comorbidity and treatment patterns.

The transformer receives unstructured clinical notes while the logistic regression baseline does not; any observed AUC-ROC advantage conflates model architecture with information access, making the hypothesis about transformer superiority untestable.
all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers including text tokenizers and vocabulary builders are fit on the full dataset before splitting, leaking test-set vocabulary and clinical text patterns into training-time preprocessing.

Stratified random sampling across all 5 years allows training and test sets to share the same temporal clinical language patterns, undermining BioBERT fine-tuning integrity for a prospectively deployed model.

Patient-level integrity is not enforced — patients with multiple index discharges over 5 years can have encounters in both training and test under random splitting, leaking patient-specific comorbidity and treatment patterns.

The transformer receives unstructured clinical notes while the logistic regression baseline does not; any observed AUC-ROC advantage conflates model architecture with information access, making the hypothesis about transformer superiority untestable.
